segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a black screen displaying 'coming up...' before transitioning to a vibrant outdoor scene at Disneyland, filled with an upbeat, playful background music. A young girl, Melody, in a light pink hat and white sweater, is with her family, including a woman in a blue jacket with green Mickey Mouse ears, a man in a black jacket holding a bag of pretzels, and another woman in a grey hoodie with a strawberry-print shirt. The group is visibly excited, laughing and interacting joyfully as they walk through the park. The woman in the blue jacket speaks directly to the camera with an excited tone, exclaiming, "You'll never guess where we are today!" and revealing that Melody is experiencing Disneyland for the first time, adding, "First timer! First time ever!" She asks Melody how excited she is, to which the girl responds with a wide smile and a playful gesture, stating her excitement is "1600%." The group shares lighthearted banter, with the man in the black jacket teasingly declaring, "Well, I'm 1601%," and joking, "You stunned her, bro! You stunned a seven-year-old! How high can she count, bro?" The woman in the grey hoodie asks Melody about her favorite Disney character, to which the girl replies, "Nothing," and when asked what she wants to see, she says, "We'll see the park." The woman in blue clarifies, "She thinks there's a park like she can actually like go out and play," while the man in black adds, "Oh, it's not a playground, girl, I've checked. We're gonna have a blast." The group discusses Melody’s plans to ride her first roller coaster, have her first beignet and churro, and get a "massive turkey leg." The setting is bright and sunny, with palm trees, colorful buildings, and the Disneyland monorail visible in the background, creating a cheerful and lively atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a group of friends at Disneyland, standing on an elevated wooden platform surrounded by lush greenery and tropical plants, offering a view of the park below. The Man in grey hoodie with glasses jokes, "I don't know if you know this, but when I was born, my mom fought a jaguar here," to which the Woman in blue jacket with green Mickey Mouse ears responds, "Are we talking about the same mother, bro?" She then exclaims, "The view is great!" and the Man in grey hoodie with glasses adds, "I'll bring Shotty up here," followed by a collective "ahhh" from the group. They then move to the Jungle Cruise ride, where the Woman in blue jacket with green Mickey Mouse ears points out "those lines down there" for the "Jungle Cruise," expressing concern, "I hope I don't get seasick," while the Man in grey hoodie with glasses jokes about getting motion sickness. On the boat, the Man in grey hoodie with glasses reacts with excitement, and the Woman in blue jacket with green Mickey Mouse ears laughs and points at the animatronic elephants amidst jungle scenery, including a large elephant figure. The Man in grey hoodie with glasses asks, "Are you okay?" and the Woman in blue jacket with green Mickey Mouse ears replies, "I'm not talking the rest of the day," later making a sound of dismay. He then points out "Jaylen," to which she responds, "Hi, that's my family," and he jokes about how awkward it would be if they weren't wearing Crocs, eliciting an "embarrassing" sound from her. After the ride, they gather near a food stand, where the Woman in blue jacket with green Mickey Mouse ears holds a Dole Whip. The group continues to enjoy the sunny day, walking through the park with palm trees and colorful buildings in the background, and the Disneyland monorail visible overhead, with the Woman in blue jacket with green Mickey Mouse ears commenting, "I got whipped." segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with RJ, the Man in grey hoodie with glasses, smiling and speaking directly to the camera in a lively Disneyland outdoor area, with guests and themed buildings in the background. He asks, "How much will you give me if I pull out the sword?", to which the Woman in grey hoodie with strawberry-print shirt responds, "A dollar!" RJ then quips, "Easiest dollar and slap in the face I've ever gotten." As he walks forward, joined by the Woman and Corey, the Man in black jacket, they approach a stone pedestal with a sword embedded in it, reminiscent of King Arthur’s Excalibur. The Woman teases RJ, "RJ, you're not that strong," to which he replies, "I like when you doubt me, it makes me feel stronger." The group laughs and jokes amidst lively park sounds and chatter as RJ attempts to pull the sword, playfully warning, "He might lose a man in front of all these people." The Woman adds, "Bro, a line forms," and Corey remarks, "RJ's scoping out his competition." RJ then points to the crowd, noting, "There is an audience for me," and mentions, "Mom's over there with the parents taking pictures." The camera pans to show the King Arthur Carrousel in the background, with its ornate design and colorful horses, as the group continues their interaction. RJ turns back to the pedestal, declaring, "Well, it's too late to back out now. My aura's on the line," while the Woman suggests, "You can step out of line, bro." RJ then says, "I'm back. Slow my drink." Corey attempts to pull the sword with effort, joking, "I just sh*t my pants," and then, "I hope Disney doesn't sue me for breaking it," as the Woman encourages, "Corey, go!" RJ then successfully pulls the sword, exclaiming, "Let's go, bro! I think it's broken," and then, "Only thing I pulled was my back," after feeling lightheaded. The Woman exclaims, "Melanie! Oh!" followed by laughter. The scene is bright and sunny, with clear blue skies, colorful flowers, and iconic Disneyland architecture, including Tudor-style buildings and the Sleeping Beauty Castle in the distance, maintaining a playful and lighthearted atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens at Disneyland near the King Arthur Carrousel, where the Woman in grey hoodie with strawberry-print shirt playfully teases the Man in grey hoodie with glasses about pulling the sword from the Stone pedestal, stating, "he was saying he was gonna pull the sword and every other girl here" and adding, "He said he shit his pants." The Man in grey hoodie with glasses responds with a joking, "Again?" The group then moves to the Mad Tea Party ride, where the Woman in blue jacket with green Mickey Mouse ears narrates, "Fifth or sixth ride, teacups," while the Woman in white top with colorful ears agrees with a "Yeah." The Man in grey hoodie with silver Mickey ears (outdoor) jokes, "The one I'm gonna throw up on," as upbeat music begins. He continues, "Oh, it goes faster than them, bro," and playfully suggests, "So let's just go the other way." The Woman in blue jacket with green Mickey Mouse ears exclaims, "Three, are you kidding?" and the Man in grey hoodie with silver Mickey ears (outdoor) jokingly complains, "No one's helping me," to which the Woman in blue jacket with green Mickey Mouse ears adds, "He don't help me," amidst group laughter. The scene shifts to a young girl in a teal jacket with Minnie Mouse ears, excitedly holding a large pink Mickey-shaped cotton candy. The Man in grey hoodie with silver Mickey ears (outdoor) asks, "What you got?" and she enthusiastically replies, "Cotton candy!" He then remarks, "Dang, that's humongous. Bigger than your face." Later, the Young girl in pink hat and white sweater asks the Woman in blue jacket with green Mickey Mouse ears, "Can I have a bite of that?" The woman offers, "Oh yeah, you want some?" The young girl thanks her with "Thanks, Mel" and "Thank you, Mel," while the Man in grey hoodie with silver Mickey ears (outdoor) declines with "I'm okay." The young girl happily takes a bite of the Dole Whip, exclaiming, "Oh my god, it's so good," and the Woman in blue jacket with green Mickey Mouse ears comments, "I was hoping she'd ask." The young girl describes the cotton candy, "And it actually feels like clouds," and the Woman in blue jacket with green Mickey Mouse ears adds, "Yeah, and then melts in your mouth." The group continues walking through the park under the Disneyland monorail track, with palm trees and colorful buildings in the background, as the Man in grey hoodie with silver Mickey ears (outdoor) suggests, "Okay, let's go get in line."

The group then transitions to the Sun Nong Dan restaurant, where the Young girl is seen drinking strawberry milk, which she sweetly describes as "just like Pocky, the pink Pocky stick," and enjoying a meal, with a close-up of a steaming pot of Korean stew. The scene shifts to a cozy indoor setting where the Woman, now in a black top, and the Man in a grey t-shirt, identified as "Corey," are seated at a desk with a computer, laughing. The Woman, now reflecting on the trip, exclaims, "Oh! Hey guys, here we are again with another missed outro!" and shares that "Disneyland was super fun," though it "took a while to edit." Corey, sipping from a red mug, playfully asks, "How was the editing?" The Woman expresses how "really fun" the editing is and how special and nostalgic the Disneyland trip was, especially as it was their "first time going to Disneyland with our baby cousin."
